节点.描述: 模块头结点，源自 @kit 注释

节点.功能描述: Provides interfaces for applications to obtain the network state, cell information, signal information, and device ID of the wireless cellular network (WCN), and provides a callback registration mechanism to listen for changes of the network, cell, and signal status of the WCN.

Obtains radio access technology (RAT) of the registered network. The system returns RAT of the packet service (PS) and circuit service (CS) domain. ranging from 0 to the maximum card slot index number supported by the device. the RAT of PS domain and CS domain of registered network. The values of RAT are as follows:
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_UNKNOWN}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_GSM}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_1XRTT}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_WCDMA}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_HSPA}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_HSPAP}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_TD_SCDMA}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_EVDO}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_EHRPD}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_LTE}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_LTE_CA}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_IWLAN}
  • {@code RadioTechnology#RADIO_TECHNOLOGY_NR}
2. Incorrect parameter types.
注释信息: @param { number } slotId - Indicates the card slot index number,
@throws { BusinessError } 401 - Parameter error. Possible causes: 1. Mandatory parameters are left unspecified.

parameters.说明: Indicates the card slot index number,
Returns

error_codes.description: Parameter error. Possible causes: 1. Mandatory parameters are left unspecified.

节点.功能描述: Checks whether the device supports 5G New Radio (NR).

节点.功能描述: Describes the radio access technology (RAT) of registered network.
Indicates radio access technology (RAT) of packet service (PS) domain.
Indicates radio access technology (RAT) of circuit service (CS) domain.
Describes the radio access technology.
Indicates unknown radio access technology (RAT).
Indicates that RAT is global system for mobile communications (GSM), including GSM, general packet radio system (GPRS), and enhanced data rates for GSM evolution (EDGE).
Indicates that RAT is code division multiple access (CDMA), including Interim Standard 95 (IS95) and Single-Carrier Radio Transmission Technology (1xRTT).
Indicates that RAT is wideband code division multiple address (WCDMA).
Indicates that RAT is high-speed packet access (HSPA), including HSPA, high-speed downlink packet access (HSDPA), and high-speed uplink packet access (HSUPA).
Indicates that RAT is evolved high-speed packet access (HSPA+), including HSPA+ and dual-carrier HSPA+ (DC-HSPA+).
Indicates that RAT is time division-synchronous code division multiple access (TD-SCDMA).
Indicates that RAT is evolution data only (EVDO), including EVDO Rev.0, EVDO Rev.A, and EVDO Rev.B.
Indicates that RAT is evolved high rate packet data (EHRPD).
Indicates that RAT is long term evolution (LTE).
Indicates that RAT is LTE carrier aggregation (LTE-CA).
Indicates that RAT is interworking WLAN (I-WLAN).
Indicates that RAT is 5G new radio (NR).
Returns child class objects specific to the network type.
Obtains the network type corresponding to the signal.
Obtains the signal level of the current network.
rsrp for LTE and NR; dbm for CDMA and EVDO; rscp for WCDMA; rssi for GSM.
Describes the network type.
Indicates unknown network type.
Indicates that the network type is GSM.
Indicates that the network type is CDMA.
Indicates that the network type is WCDMA.
Indicates that the network type is TD-SCDMA.
Indicates that the network type is LTE.
Indicates that the network type is 5G NR.
Describes the network registration state.

Obtains the operator name in the long alphanumeric format of the registered network. Returns the operator name in the long alphanumeric format as a string; returns an empty string if no operator name is obtained.

Obtains the operator name in the short alphanumeric format of the registered network. Returns the operator name in the short alphanumeric format as a string; returns an empty string if no operator name is obtained.
Obtains the PLMN code of the registered network. Returns the PLMN code as a string; returns an empty string if no operator name is obtained.
Checks whether the device is roaming.
Obtains the network registration status of the device.
Obtains the radio Access technology after config conversion.
Obtains the NSA network registration status of the device. Returns the NSA network registration status {@code NsaState}.
Obtains the status of CA. Returns {@code true} if CA is actived; returns {@code false} otherwise.
Checks whether this device is allowed to make emergency calls only. Returns {@code true} if this device is allowed to make emergency calls only; returns {@code false} otherwise.
Describes the network registration state.
Indicates a state in which a device cannot use any service.
Indicates a state in which a device can use services properly.
Indicates a state in which a device can use only the emergency call service.
Indicates that the cellular radio is powered off.
Describes the nsa state.
Indicates that a device is idle under or is connected to an LTE cell that does not support NSA.
Indicates that a device is idle under an LTE cell supporting NSA but not NR coverage detection.
Indicates that a device is connected to an LTE network under an LTE cell that supports NSA and NR coverage detection.
Indicates that a device is idle under an LTE cell supporting NSA and NR coverage detection.
Indicates that a device is connected to an LTE + NR network under an LTE cell that supports NSA.
Indicates that a device is idle under or is connected to an NG-RAN cell while being attached to 5GC.
Obtains current cell information.
Obtains the network type of the serving cell. An application can call this method to determine the network type that the child class uses.

An abstract method of the parent class whose implementation depends on the child classes. Returned child class objects vary according to the network type. Returns child class objects specific to the network type.
Obtains the network selection mode.
Indicates that the network is unavailable for registration.
Indicates that the network is unavailable for registration.
Manual network selection modes.
